title: Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025 is a proposed bill aimed at protecting human rights in the face of increasingly prevalent artificial intelligence systems. Its exact scope and provisions are not specified, but the title suggests a focus on ensuring that AI development and deployment do not infringe on individuals' fundamental rights and freedoms.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 is a policy document that aims to regulate and mitigate the environmental effects of artificial intelligence. The exact details of the policy are not available, but it likely addresses the environmental consequences resulting from the development and deployment of AI technologies.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This Act aims to establish governance for Artificial Intelligence in various sectors to ensure its safe and responsible use. The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 likely outlines regulations and guidelines for the development, deployment, and oversight of AI technologies across different industries.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 18 - 15.
summary: The K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at enhancing education in AI for school-aged children. It likely focuses on promoting knowledge and preparedness in artificial intelligence among students in the K-12 age group.

title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act is a proposed law that aims to temporarily halt the construction of large artificial intelligence data centers. The purpose of this act is to regulate and manage the rapid growth of AI data infrastructure.

title: SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.
summary: The SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026 aims to promote and support the adoption of artificial intelligence technology within Small Business Administration programs. This act likely requires the SBA to report on or track the implementation of AI within their operations.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: This act aims to protect families from high energy costs associated with data centers that utilize artificial intelligence. It likely targets policies to reduce or mitigate the financial burden on families.

title: Conversational AI Services Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Conversational AI Services Act is a policy document outlining regulations and guidance for the use of conversational AI services. Its purpose is likely to establish standards, protect user rights, and govern how conversational AI technology can be developed and deployed.

title: Homestake AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: The Homestake AI Act of 2026 is a proposed policy document; its exact details are unknown, but it likely relates to the regulation or legislation of artificial intelligence. It may outline new rules or guidelines for the development, use, and governance of AI technology.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a proposed policy aimed at regulating the use and development of artificial intelligence for defense and national security purposes. This policy likely aims to address potential risks and ethics associated with AI technology in defense.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: This document appears to be a policy outline for the utilization of Artificial Intelligence by the Small Business Administration (SBA) in 2026. The purpose of the policy is likely to guide the implementation of AI within the SBA.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 is a policy document, but more information is needed to provide a clear summary. However, based on the title, it likely relates to policies and regulations regarding the use of Artificial Intelligence in farming, agriculture, and rural management.

title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act aims to establish a framework for artificial intelligence leadership and uniformity in the US. The purpose of this act is unclear without further information or description.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: This policy aims to prevent AI-powered companies from inflating prices and fixing wages, protecting consumers and workers from unfair business practices. The law targets the misuse of artificial intelligence in setting prices and compensating employees.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a proposed law aimed at preventing the theft of American artificial intelligence models. It likely involves measures to protect and safeguard the intellectual property rights of AI developers and creators in the United States.

title: Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.
summary: This policy document outlines the Research and Oversight of AI in Courts Act of 2026, likely aiming to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in court systems to ensure fairness and transparency. The legislation probably involves oversight and research to monitor the impact of AI on court proceedings.

title: Protecting Consumers from Deceptive AI Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: This act aims to protect consumers from deceptive uses of artificial intelligence. It likely addresses the potential misuse of AI technology in consumer transactions and products.

title: Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Science, Space, and Technology.
summary: The Federal Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Act of 2026 is a US law aimed at managing the risks associated with Artificial Intelligence. Its purpose is to establish guidelines and regulations for the development and use of AI systems in the country.

title: Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act
action: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 619.
summary: The Unleashing AI Innovation in Financial Services Act aims to promote and enable the use of artificial intelligence in the financial services sector. This is likely to involve creating a supportive regulatory environment that encourages innovation while maintaining consumer protection and financial stability.
